latest.title: Renewal of Contract
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 3867T
Crimson Tide PLC
18 February 2026
REACH: non-regulatory announcement*
18 February 2026
Crimson Tide plc
("Crimson Tide", the "Company" or the "Group")
Renewal of Contract
Crimson Tide plc (AIM: TIDE), the provider of the mpro5 operational compliance platform, is pleased to announce the renewal of a 36‑month contract with Koenig & Bauer (UK) Ltd ("
Koenig & Bauer
"), part of Koenig & Bauer AG, one of the world's oldest and most established printing press manufacturers.
Koenig & Bauer has been a valued customer of Crimson Tide since the early years of the Company's commercial operations. Over this time, they have adopted mpro5 in a highly specialised and unique way, integrating the platform deeply into their service and operational workflows. Their renewed commitment reflects the long‑standing partnership between the two companies and the continued relevance of mpro5 in supporting Koenig & Bauer's operational excellence.
Commenting on the renewal, Jon Clarke, CEO of Crimson Tide plc, said:
"We are delighted that Koenig & Bauer has chosen to renew their contract with us for a further 36 months. As one of our earliest adopters, they exemplify the long-term value that mpro5 can deliver, particularly when used in innovative and specialised ways. This renewal reinforces the strength of our long-standing relationship and supports our focus on sustainable, recurring growth."
Enquiries:
Crimson Tide plc

latest.title: Change of Non-Executive Director
latest.content:
23 Jan 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 0459Q
Crimson Tide PLC
23 January 2026
23 January 2026
Crimson Tide plc
("Crimson Tide", the "Company" or the "Group")
Change of Non-Executive Director
Crimson Tide, the provider of the mpro5 process management app, announces that Janet Morris has tendered her resignation as a Non-Executive Director of the Company, which will take effect immediately.
The Company had already initiated a process to bring new non-executive skills to its Board and will, accordingly, accelerate that process and make a further announcement as soon as practicable.
Chris Fielding, Chair of the Company, commented: "The Board would like to place on record its thanks for Janet's valuable contribution to the Company and wishes her every success in her future endeavours".

latest.title: Customer Contracts Update
latest.content:
24 Dec 2025 07:00
RNS Number : 7312M
Crimson Tide PLC
24 December 2025
THIS ANNOUNCEMENT CONTAINS INSIDE INFORMATION AS STIPULATED UNDER THE MARKET ABUSE REGULATION NO. 596/2014 (AS INCORPORATED INTO UK LAW BY VIRTUE OF THE EUROPEAN UNION (WITHDRAWAL) ACT 2018 AS AMENDED BY VIRTUE OF THE MARKET ABUSE (AMENDMENT) (EU EXIT) REGULATIONS 2019). UPON THE PUBLICATION OF THIS ANNOUNCEMENT VIA A REGULATORY INFORMATION SERVICE, THIS INSIDE INFORMATION IS NOW CONSIDERED TO BE IN THE PUBLIC DOMAIN
24 December 2025
Crimson Tide plc
("Crimson Tide", the "Company" or the "Group")
Customer Contracts Update
​
Crimson Tide plc (AIM:TIDE), the provider of the mpro5 process management app, announces that it has received formal notice from a significant customer, a major retailer (the "
Customer
"), that it intends to exercise a break clause in its existing contract with the Company. The break clause will take effect on 31 March 2026. To note, this does not relate to the contract renewal which was announced on 10 November 2025.
The Company remains in constructive dialogue with the Customer regarding transition arrangements and is implementing mitigation strategies to reduce the impact of this change.
Whilst the Company is disappointed to lose a valued customer, this change will release considerable resources previously dedicated to servicing this account. The Customer's requirements necessitated significant bespoke development within the platform, which has constrained product development capacity. The conclusion of this contract will enable the Company to redeploy resources towards higher-margin opportunities, accelerate delivery of core product enhancements and support the Company's strategy to build a more scalable SaaS platform. Whilst there will be an immediate impact on revenue, the Board believes that this transition will benefit the Company over the longer term.
The contract, which commenced on 1 December 2024, represented approximately 12% of the Company's annual recurring revenue ("
ARR
") for the financial year ended 30 April 2025 and 30 April 2026, albeit at below average margins for the reasons explained above.
The Company has separately secured renewal agreements with two customers, representing combined monthly recurring revenue ("
MRR
") of approximately £14,700 for 12 months.
Both customers had previously served notice of non-renewal and were therefore classified as churned. Following a renewed focus by the current management team on customer retention, and the implementation of a structured retention strategy focused on maximising customer value from mpro5, the Company has successfully re-engaged and retained both customers.
Enquiries:
Crimson Tide plc
+ 44 1892 542444
Chris Fielding, Non-Executive Chair
Jon Clarke, Chief Executive Officer
Rachael Rowe, Finance Director
Allenby Capital Limited - Nominated Adviser & Broker

company_30s.what_they_do: Crimson Tide Plc supplies high‑performance, modular water‑cooling systems for data‑centre and high‑end gaming hardware. They design, manufacture and distribute liquid‑cooling blocks, pumps, radiators and custom cooling solutions that help servers and PCs run cooler and quieter.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global data‑centre market is expanding at about 8% a year, driven by cloud growth and AI workloads that need reliable, low‑power cooling. Crimson Tide’s products plug directly into existing server racks, giving operators a quick, cost‑effective way to improve thermal efficiency without major infrastructure changes.
company_30s.current_state: The company has moved from a niche niche into a broader data‑centre focus. After a period of high volatility and thin trading, it has secured a multi‑year NHS contract renewal, appointed a new CEO and finance director, and released a full‑year audit that shows a modest improvement in revenue. The share price is trading at a fraction of its 52‑week high, reflecting a steep decline that many investors see as a capitulation point.

rns_narrative: Crimson Tide has been busy renewing and expanding its client base. The February contract renewal with a major public‑sector customer signals a steady revenue stream that will likely underpin future earnings.
Leadership changes – a new CEO, a new finance director and a new chair – indicate a fresh strategy focus on scaling operations and improving profitability.
The company has also released audited results and updated financial statements, showing that it has survived the downturn and is now on a more stable cash‑flow footing.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Cautiously optimistic – people are watching the contract renewal, but liquidity remains thin.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders point to the new contract and fresh leadership as a sign that the company is ready to grow. They note that the new CEO’s track record suggests a turnaround could happen.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others warn that the price is still far below intrinsic value, that trading volume is negligible, and that the company’s market cap is too small to sustain a major rebound. They fear that any misstep could leave the share stuck in a low‑liquidity trap.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The tone has shifted from pure pessimism to a mix of guarded hope after the contract renewal announcement. The chatter is still sparse, but the few bullish posts are more frequent now.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends data is flat – no recent spikes in search activity. That means the market is largely unaware of any upcoming news, giving a contrarian a surprise edge.

near_term_catalysts: The official contract renewal announcement on Feb 18 2026, which will confirm a new revenue stream and likely lift sentiment.
The upcoming AGM results on Oct 31 2025 – a better‑than‑expected profit margin or a clear path to profitability could spark a bounce.
Any new product launch or partnership announcement that expands the company’s reach beyond the NHS market.
A volume spike that breaks the current 50‑day moving‑average support level, indicating institutional buying.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a clear break above the 52‑week low with a 10‑day volume spike. A sudden spike in new share issuance or a positive audit note would also confirm the bullish thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The share is still in the early stages of a recovery cycle. The APEX score suggests moderate compression, so a mean‑reversion is plausible if the above catalysts materialise within the next 2–3 months.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The share sits at a deep crash bottom. Historical data shows that deep‑crash signals (green or orange) deliver average returns of 40–110% with win rates around 40–50%. If the company’s new contract and leadership drive a 50% rebound to half of its 52‑week high, that would represent a 100%+ upside – a classic high‑reward, low‑downside play.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: If liquidity stays thin or if the new contract fails to materialise, the price could stay stuck at the current low. The worst‑case scenario is a flat or slightly declining price, which would leave the investor with a small loss if a position is entered at the current level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside of 100–160% versus a downside of essentially the current price (the worst you lose is the amount invested if the stock never bounces). The asymmetry is roughly 3:1 or better.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a breakout above the 52‑week low with a 10‑day volume spike. Use a 2‑day stop below the low and size the position so that a 20% drop would wipe out 10% of the account.
playbook.if_watching: Wait for a volume spike of at least 5× the average daily volume and a price move of 5% above the low. If the contract renewal announcement is followed by a 10% jump, consider entering.
playbook.if_passing: Not applicable – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Crimson Tide’s steep crash, fresh contract renewal and new leadership create a classic contrarian play with high upside and minimal downside if the mean‑reversion starts within the next few months.
